one_line_takeaway: Comprehensive empirical evidence that classifier-based safety gates for self-improving AI cannot simultaneously bound cumulative false-accepts and keep improving (18 configs, incl. a 100%-train-accuracy MLP, all fail), while an O(d) Lipschitz-ball parameter-space verifier achieves delta=0 with 100% soundness to d=17,408 and chains to unbounded verified improvement — with the honest caveat that at LLM scale the delta=0 becomes conditional on estimated Lipschitz bounds.
key_finding: Classification fails structurally, not for lack of capacity: all 18 configs fail the dual conditions, and a deep MLP with 100% training accuracy still accumulates cumulative false-accept mass Sum delta >= 52 at test time; even the Gaussian Bayes-optimal classifier and the NP-optimal test fail, and failure persists at Delta_s=2.0 (per-step delta=0.185, TPR=0.864) because delta>0 is constant so Sum delta diverges. Verification escapes it: the O(d) Lipschitz-ball check gives delta=0 with 100% soundness across all seven dimensions (d=84..17,408) using provably analytical bounds, and ball chaining converts a single verified region into unbounded verified traversal — +4.31±0.08 reward on Reacher-v4 with delta=0 while total displacement reaches 17.2x the first ball radius (and the safety margin actually GROWS as the system improves). Crucially the LLM-scale result is honestly framed as validating the compositional Lipschitz MECHANISM, not the safety oracle, and its delta=0 is CONDITIONAL on estimated Lipschitz constants being valid upper bounds — unlike the unconditional delta=0 at d<=17,408.
core_contribution: An empirical companion to a separate theory paper ([12], Scrivens 'Information-theoretic limits...'). The theorems it validates (the classification impossibility Theorem 1, the verification escape Theorem 2, and the finite-horizon ceiling Theorem 5) are NOT this paper's own results — they belong to [12], though Appendix B.1 reproduces a self-contained proof of Theorem 1. This paper's own standalone contributions are three: (1) a systematic negative result showing 18 classifier-based safety-gate configurations plus 3 safe-RL paradigms all fail the 'dual conditions' for safe self-improvement (bounded cumulative false-accept risk AND unbounded accepted improvements) on a d=240 Liquid Time-Constant controller, three MuJoCo environments, and controlled Gaussian separations; (2) a validated parameter-space 'Lipschitz ball' verification protocol (Algorithm 1, ball chaining) that achieves false-accept rate delta=0 with 100% empirical soundness from d=84 to d=17,408 and scales to a 1.26M-trainable-parameter Qwen2.5-7B LoRA run; and (3) empirically measured scaling constants theory alone did not predict (optimal mutation scale sigma* proportional to d^-0.54, distribution separations Delta_s in [0.059,0.091] on MuJoCo, classifiers at 36-58% of the Hoelder ceiling).
threat_model: An iteratively self-modifying AI system proposes parameter mutations that are filtered by a cheap binary safety gate before a ground-truth oracle would catch them. A learned classifier gate (red-team evaluator, thresholded reward model, learned discriminator) is assumed to sit between the system and unsafe self-modifications over hundreds-to-thousands of iterations. The security failure: because safe and unsafe modification distributions overlap, any classifier's per-step false-accept rate delta_n cannot be driven summable (Sum delta_n < infinity, needed via Borel-Cantelli to guarantee a positive probability of NEVER passing an unsafe mutation) while still accepting infinitely many safe improvements (Sum TPR_n = infinity). Adversarial/worst-case mutation crafting is explicitly out of scope (limitation 8). The paper argues the safe design is to replace classification gates with parameter-space verification (accept a mutation only if it lies within a provably-safe Lipschitz ball around a known-safe point).
limitations: (1) The core theorems are the companion paper's, not this paper's — this is validation, and much of the 'universal bound' framing rests on [12]. (2) Delta=0 is only UNCONDITIONAL up to d=17,408; at LLM scale it is conditional on finite-difference Lipschitz estimates (5x safety factor) being true upper bounds — the authors flag computing tractable closed-form constants at LLM scale as the key open problem. (3) The guarantee is D-safety (safety on a fixed operating domain), not universal safety. (4) The negative result is against classifiers at their 'natural operating thresholds'; with FULL oracle access CPO and shielding also reach delta=0 (they effectively run the oracle), so the real claim is a ~2,000-20,476x cost advantage, not that classifiers can never be safe. (5) Constrained chaining is not perfectly clean: 1 of 5 seeds produced 6 false accepts out of 400 checks (0.35% rate), so the 'delta=0' headline holds only 4/5 seeds there. (6) No adversarial/worst-case mutation analysis; safety margins can shrink toward zero under chaining; model scale capped at 7.6B and environments are standard small testbeds (d=240-1,824). (7) The LLM safety oracle (10-50 adversarial prompts) validates the mechanism, not production safety — the 50-prompt oracle caught 20 violations the 10-prompt one missed, and left the model 'not safe at end' (margin 0.98).

datasets_used: Liquid Time-Constant (LTC) controller, d=240, on a 2D point-mass obstacle-avoidance navigation task (primary testbed)
Synthetic Gaussian data (d=50) with controlled distribution separation Delta_s in {0.5,1.0,1.5,2.0}
Feedforward MLP controllers (MLP-8-8 d=130 .. MLP-64-64 d=4610) for architecture generalization
Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct LoRA fine-tuning (7.6B params, rank-4 LoRA on q/v proj = 1,261,568 trainable params), 10-prompt and 50-prompt adversarial safety oracles
quantitative_results: 18 classifier configurations (3 task-specific gates + 15 classifier configs) ALL fail the dual conditions over N=500 iterations
Deep 3-layer MLP reaches 100% training accuracy yet still accumulates cumulative Sum delta >= 52 at test time
Gate A static MLP: mean delta=0.37 (fitted p=0.26<1); Gate B retrained: becomes vacuous, rejects everything after step 42; Gate C CBF-adaptive: mean delta=0.09, p~0.02
Even at Delta_s=2.0 the best classifier has per-step delta=0.185, TPR=0.864 but still fails because Sum delta diverges; utility gap to ball verifier still 5.3x
Lipschitz ball: 100% soundness at every dimension d=84 to d=17,408; optimal mutation scale sigma* proportional to d^-0.54
2D ball construction (d=240): 500/500 accepted, delta=0, TPR=1.0, gate 0.01 ms vs oracle 410 ms = 20,476x speedup
Ball chaining 2D: +10.4%±0.4% (n=5), delta=0, displacement 3.5±0.7x first ball radius
Ball chaining Reacher-v4: +4.31±0.08 reward (n=5), delta=0, displacement 17.2±6.9x radius; margin GROWS with improvement
Constrained chaining: +3.21±0.02 reward, delta=0 on 4/5 seeds; 1 seed had 6 false accepts / 400 checks (0.35% rate)

Qwen2.5-7B LoRA (10-prompt oracle): 79% ball acceptance (158/200), 0 oracle rejections, 42 chain transitions, loss 9.64->1.75 (81.8% down), total displacement 234x ball radius (r=0.0110), 4.8x wall-clock speedup

Qwen2.5-7B LoRA (50-prompt oracle): 75.5% ball acceptance (151/200), caught 20 safety violations invisible to the 10-prompt oracle, saved 7,550 prompt evaluations, model 'not safe at end' (margin 0.98)
Finite-horizon ceiling: at N=10,000, B=0.01, classifier utility <=12.4 vs ball 5,000 (>=403x); classifier utility grows exp(O(sqrt(log N))), ball grows linearly
Compositional per-group verification: safe radius up to 37x larger than full-network ball when only LTC time-constants are modified
baselines_compared: Classifier families: Logistic Regression, k-NN (k=5), Random Forest, Linear SVM, Gaussian/Optimal Bayes (full covariance), deep 3-layer MLP
Task-specific gates: static MLP (Gate A), retrained MLP (Gate B), CBF-adaptive hybrid (Gate C)
Safe RL paradigms: CPO-style [Achiam et al. 2017], Lyapunov-style [Berkenkamp et al. 2017], safety shielding [Alshiekh et al. 2018] — under both partial rollouts and full oracle access
Proposed methods: Lipschitz ball verifier (sigma-matched and sigma=sigma*), ball chaining
